author: Dr. Sarah Patel (MICU resident) / Dr. Daniel Foster (MICU attending)
content:
MICU DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — 2/22/2027, 7:30 AM (Hospital Day 8)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
Day #6 mechanical ventilation, Day #8 admission.
Interpreter: Vietnamese phone interpreter used for family update this morning.

24-HOUR EVENTS:
- Overnight: hemodynamically stable. Off all vasopressors x 2 days. Tmax 99.4 F (defervescing well).
- Sedation: minimized propofol overnight; RASS goal -2 to 0; tolerating; intermittently responsive to verbal command via interpreter.
- Vent settings: weaned to AC volume control, Vt 320, PEEP 10 (down from 12), FiO2 0.45 (down from 0.55), RR 18. P/F ratio 168 (improving from 140). Plateau pressure 22; driving pressure 12.
- Repeat ABG: pH 7.38 / pCO2 36 / pO2 76 / HCO3 22.
- Spontaneous breathing trial attempted at 5 AM — passed initial 30-min portion (RSBI 78); failed at 60 minutes with increasing respiratory rate to 32. Stopped and returned to AC. Will retry tomorrow.
- Tolerating tube feeds (Nutren) at 30 mL/h target rate (down from 45 to 30 yesterday due to mild gastric residual; today residuals minimal). Will up-titrate.

SYSTEMS:

NEURO: Sedation lightened. Following commands intermittently. No focal deficits. Eyes open spontaneously when name called. CAM-ICU positive intermittently for hyperactive features (pulling at lines) — adding scheduled low-dose haloperidol PRN and continuing to minimize sedation.

RESPIRATORY: Moderate ARDS, improving. P/F 168 today. CXR (AM) improving aeration of bilateral lower lobes, persisting small bilateral pleural effusions, ETT in good position. Plan: continue weaning vent, daily SBT, prone positioning not currently indicated given improving P/F.

CARDIOVASCULAR: Off vasopressors. Sinus rhythm; HR 78-92. BP 120-138 systolic. Lactate cleared 4 days ago.

GI / NUTRITION: Bowel sounds present. Tube feeds advancing. Stools normal. Liver function continues to improve: AST 48 (from peak 124), ALT 62 (from peak 158), ALP 88 (from peak 142), T-bili 1.6 (from peak 4.1, down). Lipase 240 (from peak 1124, down).

RENAL: AKI improving. Cr 1.6 today (down from peak 2.6). Adequate UOP (1.2 mL/kg/h). Holding lisinopril and any nephrotoxins. Holding contrast.

ID / SEPSIS: ALL THREE AFB SMEARS RETURNED NEGATIVE. NAAT (Xpert MTB/RIF) ALSO NEGATIVE — active TB effectively ruled out. AFB cultures still incubating x 8 weeks. AIRBORNE ISOLATION DISCONTINUED today after huddle with ID. Pip/tazo continues, day 7 of 10-14 day course; afebrile x 3 days, WBC down to 9.2 from peak 19.8. Plan to step down to oral ciprofloxacin once tolerating PO (still NPO).

HEME: Hgb 8.4 (chronic anemia + ICU dilution). No active bleeding. Plt 312. INR 1.2 (off heparin gtt; on subcutaneous heparin prophylaxis for VTE).

ENDOCRINE: Sliding-scale insulin; glucose 90-150 range. Stress hyperglycemia resolving.

MSK / SKIN: Skin intact, regular turning. Heels offloaded. PT involved (passive ROM, in-bed).

PSYCH / FAMILY: Son Quang at bedside daily; daughter-in-law Linh and grandchildren visit on weekends. Family meeting yesterday with Vietnamese interpreter — discussed expected ventilator weaning trajectory and the need for likely tracheostomy if not extubated within 1-2 more weeks. Family understood. Code status FULL CODE (re-confirmed).

INCIDENTAL FINDING — 2.5 CM RUL LUNG NODULE: Documented in pulmonary consult 2/18 and ID consult 2/19. Active TB ruled out as above.

PROBLEMS / PLAN:
#1 ARDS, moderate, resolving — continue lung-protective vent, daily SBT, anticipate trach by next week if not extubated.
#2 Severe acute biliary pancreatitis, post-ERCP, improving — labs trending down, no anatomic abscess on ultrasound 2/20.
#3 Septic shock (E. coli biliary source) resolved — continue pip/tazo through ~2/28; reassess for PO step-down.
#4 AKI on CKD — recovering; Cr 1.6 today.
#5 Cholelithiasis — surgery deferred until clinically stable; general surgery rounding.
#6 Latent TB infection — active TB ruled out (3 AFB negative, NAAT negative). Airborne isolation discontinued.
#7 RUL lung nodule, 2.5 cm — see above.
#8 HTN — held lisinopril; will resume when Cr at baseline.
#9 HLD — atorvastatin held in ICU, will resume on transfer out.
#10 Mild ICU delirium — minimize sedation, low-dose haloperidol PRN, frequent reorientation in Vietnamese via interpreter.
#11 VTE prophylaxis — heparin SC q8h; mechanical SCDs.
#12 Nutrition — enteral via NG, advancing toward goal 60 mL/h.
#13 Family communication — daily updates via Vietnamese interpreter to son Quang.

Anticipated remaining ICU course: ~7-14 days for ventilator weaning. Anticipate floor transfer when extubated and off antibiotics.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Sarah Patel, MD (MICU resident)
Dr. Daniel Foster, MD (MICU attending)
Date: 2/22/2027
